personality_belief.metaphysical_profile: Used only as cultural flavor, never as a decision rule.
personality_belief.decision_style: practical and constrained by real workflow trade-offs
personality_belief.trust_orientation: needs proof from concrete workflows, not just positioning
personality_belief.self_image: responsible, busy, and careful about adding complexity

economic_profile.purchase_decision_process: tests fit against current workflow before committing

life_goals: stay on top of work without constant catch-up
fears: paying for another tool that becomes shelfware
data leakage in shared family/work spaces
moral_boundaries: will not trade away other people's privacy for convenience
status_concerns: does not want to appear disorganised in front of clients or team
wants to be seen as efficient, not just busy

life_story.childhood_background: Grew up in a household that valued practical decisions and financial caution in a hong kong smb context.
life_story.education_journey: Completed diploma level education and learned through a mix of formal training and on-the-job repetition during a mid career operator phase.
life_story.career_path: Moved into program manager work because it rewards reliability and the ability to keep moving parts aligned. Workflow maturity is currently tool heavy. Iâve spent years building spreadsheets that eventually broke under their own weight, so now Iâm wary of 'smart' tools that require complex setup.
life_story.family_story: Currently single parent with one child (age 6), living in Shatin. The high caregiving load means my work hours are often fragmentedâearly morning, late evening, or weekends. This makes 'always-on' apps stressful rather than helpful; I need tools that respect my limited windows of focus.
life_story.current_daily_routine: Most weekdays are split between coordination work, message follow-up, and protecting small windows of focused time. I start the day checking WhatsApp and email in Cantonese/English mix, then switch to deep work. Decision speed is committee slow because I often have to align with a partner or client before committing, and purchase authority is shared household deciderâI check bank statements together.
recent_life_events: recently reviewed software spend after noticing duplicate subscriptions
trying to reduce context-switching during the week
reassessing workflow due to medium cash-flow volatility and variable employment stability
frustrations: too many tools causing tab fatigue
manual follow-up via WhatsApp getting lost in chats
context scattered across messages, emails, and Excel sheets
family responsibilities make admin work feel heavier when I'm tired
workflow habits break when too many exceptions pile up
hidden_needs: wants confidence that a new tool will simplify the week quickly
needs a reason to trust the workflow before sharing real work into it
needs a way to separate 'work mode' from 'home mode' mentally

behavior_profile.buying_behavior: waits until value is concrete enough to justify habit change. I will try a free trial, but if it takes more than 30 minutes to set up, I abandon it.
information_sources: peer referrals from LinkedIn connections in HK tech/PM circles
product demos that show specific HK-based use cases
comparison notes against tools I already use
social_media_usage: WhatsApp groups for quick team coordination and family logistics
behavior_profile.referral_influence: moderate to high. If a trusted colleague says it works, I listen. If they say it's 'too much admin', I skip it.
brand_trust_signals: specific user stories from similar roles
emotional_triggers: feeling more in control of follow-up
seeing a 'zero inbox' state maintained by the tool

active_pain_points: manual follow-up via WhatsApp leading to missed deadlines
context scattered across messages, emails, and local files
family responsibilities make admin work feel heavier when multitasking
workflow habits break when too many exceptions pile up
latent_pain_points: mental load from tracking too many loose ends
slow drift between promises made in chat and actual follow-up
anxiety about missing a critical update while with child
jobs_to_be_done: capture next actions reliably from mixed-language chats
create a single source of truth for project status

sensitive_reality_layer.social_risk_profile: does not want to look careless with colleagues' or clients' information, especially given the blurred lines between work and home life in a small household.
sensitive_reality_layer.fairness_and_inclusion_profile: expects product language to remain respectful and not assume one type of user. Prefers neutral, professional tone.
response_boundaries: will not share private third-party data without a clear reason
will not discuss salary or specific financial details openly
sensitive_reality_layer.public_explanation_preference: plain language with concrete examples, avoiding jargon or hype.

audit_evidence_layer.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.
